description: Rules proposed automatically by the Phase LEARN pipeline based on trending rubric-criterion failures. Every rule here was drafted by an LLM, then human-reviewed and approved before landing. Empty by design until the first proposal is approved.

examples: BAD: A text input for an email address simply clears its value or prevents submission without showing *why* it's invalid.
GOOD: An email input with an invalid value displays a red border and the text 'Invalid email format' appears in red text directly beneath it.
guidelines.rule: For any user input field with validation, display a specific error message directly below the invalid input field and apply a distinct visual style (e.g., a red border) to the input field itself when validation fails.
guidelines.why: The INPUT_VALIDATION_FEEDBACK criterion has a 100% fail rate across 5 recent evaluations, indicating that generated mini-apps consistently lack clear user feedback for invalid input.
